revision.text:
'''巽神社'''（ちゃつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]]にある[[神社]]。
==概要== *祭神 [[応神天皇]] **合祀 印色入日子命ほか5柱 *由緒 創建年代については不明であるが、かつては[[河内国]]渋川郡大地村に鎮座し、八幡神社と称されていた。明治時代の末期に行われた神社の統廃合により、周辺の横野神社（[[式内社]]）などが合祀され、社名が現在の巽神社と改名された。 *所在地 大阪府大阪市生野区巽南3-17-19
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/kawati/tatumi.htm 神奈備HP（巽神社）] *[http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]
[[category:大阪府の神社|たつみ]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]]にある[[神社]]。
==概要== *祭神 [[応神天皇]] **合祀 印色入日子命ほか5柱 *由緒 創建年代については不明であるが、かつては[[河内国]]渋川郡大地村に鎮座し、八幡神社と称されていた。明治時代の末期に行われた神社の統廃合により、周辺の横野神社（[[式内社]]）などが合祀され、社名が現在の巽神社と改名された。 *所在地 大阪府大阪市生野区巽南3-17-19
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/kawati/tatumi.htm 神奈備HP（巽神社）] *[http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]
[[category:大阪府の神社|たつみ]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]]巽南にある[[神社]]。
==概要== *祭神 [[応神天皇]] **合祀 印色入日子命ほか5柱 *由緒 創建年代については不明であるが、かつては[[河内国]]渋川郡大地村に鎮座し、八幡神社と称されていた。明治時代の末期に行われた神社の統廃合により、周辺の横野神社（[[式内社]]）などが合祀され、社名が現在の巽神社と改名された。 *所在地 大阪府大阪市生野区巽南3-17-19
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/kawati/tatumi.htm 神奈備HP（巽神社）] *[http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]
[[category:大阪府の神社|たつみ]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: fmt,追記
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=巽神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市生野区巽南3丁目17-19| 祭神=応神天皇| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]}}
'''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]]巽南にある[[神社]]である。
[[応神天皇]]を主[[祭神]]とし、印色入日子命・[[イザナギ|伊弉册尊]]・[[天常立尊]]・[[天照大神]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。
==歴史== 明治40年4月27日、旧[[渋川郡]][[巽村]]内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（大地字印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・伊賀ケ字伊賀ケの天神杜・西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・四条字山小路の天神杜）を[[神社合祀|合祀]]し、村の名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。
===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、享保16年2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/kawati/tatumi.htm 巽神社]（神奈備） *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/041102-01.html 横野神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:たつみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] {{shinto-stub}}

===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、享保16年2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/kawati/tatumi.htm 巽神社]（神奈備） *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/041102-01.html 横野神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]

revision.comment: 座標
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=巽神社| 所在地=大阪府大阪市生野区巽南三丁目17番19号| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP}}| 祭神=応神天皇| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月14日]]}}
'''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]]巽南三丁目にある[[神社]]である。
[[応神天皇]]を主[[祭神]]とし、印色入日子命・[[イザナギ|伊弉册尊]]・[[天常立尊]]・[[天照大神]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。
==歴史== [[1907年]]（明治40年）4月27日、[[中河内郡]]（旧[[渋川郡]]）[[巽町|巽村]]内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（[[大字]]大地[[小字|字]]印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・大字伊加賀字伊加賀の天神杜・大字西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・大字矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・大字四条字山小路の天神杜）を[[神社合祀|合祀]]し、村名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。
===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、[[1731年]]（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]
{{デフォルトソート:たつみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:生野区]] {{Shinto-stub}}

===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、[[1731年]]（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.osaka.jp/ikuno/spot/temple.html#tatsumi 生野区ホームページ]
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:たつみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]]

===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、[[1731年]]（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[生野区]]巽南三丁目17番19号 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小） | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月15日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=巽神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]][[巽南]]三丁目にある[[神社]]である。 [[応神天皇]]を主[[祭神]]とし、印色入日子命・[[イザナギ|伊弉册尊]]・[[天常立尊]]・[[天照大神]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== [[1907年]]（明治40年）4月27日、[[中河内郡]]（旧[[渋川郡]]）[[巽町|巽村]]内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（[[大字]]大地[[小字|字]]印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・大字伊加賀字伊加賀の天神杜・大字西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・大字矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・大字四条字山小路の天神杜）を[[神社合祀|合祀]]し、村名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。 ===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、[[1731年]]（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html 巽神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:たつみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[JA:巽神社 (大阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479957}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|巽神社 (大阪市)|version=107519468|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称=巽神社 | 画像=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[生野区]]巽南三丁目17番19号 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小） | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月15日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=巽神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]][[巽南]]三丁目にある[[神社]]である。 [[応神天皇]]を主[[祭神]]とし、印色入日子命・[[イザナギ|伊弉册尊]]・[[天常立尊]]・[[天照大神]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== [[1907年]]（明治40年）4月27日、[[中河内郡]]（旧[[渋川郡]]）[[巽町|巽村]]内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（[[大字]]大地[[小字|字]]印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・大字伊加賀字伊加賀の天神杜・大字西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・大字矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・大字四条字山小路の天神杜）を[[神社合祀|合祀]]し、村名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。 ===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、[[1731年]]（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html 巽神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]]

鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[生野区]]巽南三丁目17番19号 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小） | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月15日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=巽神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪市]][[生野区]][[巽南]]三丁目にある[[神社]]である。 [[応神天皇]]を主[[祭神]]とし、印色入日子命・[[イザナギ|伊弉册尊]]・[[天常立尊]]・[[天照大神]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[菅原道真]]を配祀する。 ==歴史== [[1907年]]（明治40年）4月27日、[[中河内郡]]（旧[[渋川郡]]）[[巽町|巽村]]内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（[[大字]]大地[[小字|字]]印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・大字伊加賀字伊加賀の天神杜・大字西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・大字矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・大字四条字山小路の天神杜）を[[神社合祀|合祀]]し、村名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。 ===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、[[1731年]]（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を[[垂仁天皇]]の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『[[日本書紀]]』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。 == 外部リンク == * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html 巽神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tatsumi Shrine Osaka'''|巽神社 (大阪市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

former site|WD=Q135185614}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府大阪市生野区巽南三丁目17番19号', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:巽神社 (大阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=巽神社 | 画像=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|生野区|Q490520}}巽南三丁目17番19号 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=巽神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|生野区|Q490520}}{{jalink|巽南|Q75830394}}三丁目にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}を主{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}とし、印色入日子命・{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉册尊}}・{{jalink|天常立尊}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}・{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を配祀する。 ===歴史=== {{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）4月27日、{{jalink|中河内郡|Q11366394}}（旧{{jalink|渋川郡|Q11561609}}）{{jalink|巽町|Q11479954|巽村}}内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（{{jalink|大字|Q424857}}大地{{jalink|小字|Q5327509|字}}印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・大字伊加賀字伊加賀の天神杜・大字西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・大字矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・大字四条字山小路の天神杜）を{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}し、村名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。 ===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、{{jalink|1731年|Q6963}}（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。 === 外部リンク === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html 巽神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:たつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]]

former site|WD=Q135185614}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府大阪市生野区巽南三丁目17番19号', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:巽神社 (大阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=巽神社 | 画像=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|生野区|Q490520}}巽南三丁目17番19号 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小） | 本殿={{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=巽神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''巽神社'''（たつみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪市|Q35765}}{{jalink|生野区|Q490520}}{{jalink|巽南|Q75830394}}三丁目にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}を主{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}とし、印色入日子命・{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉册尊}}・{{jalink|天常立尊}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}・{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を配祀する。 ===歴史=== {{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）4月27日、{{jalink|中河内郡|Q11366394}}（旧{{jalink|渋川郡|Q11561609}}）{{jalink|巽町|Q11479954|巽村}}内にあった八幡神社（祭神 応神天皇）に、村内の5社（{{jalink|大字|Q424857}}大地{{jalink|小字|Q5327509|字}}印地の横野神社（式内社。祭神 印色入日子命）・大字伊加賀字伊加賀の天神杜・大字西足代字葭の内の天照皇大神社・大字矢柄字宮の前の熊野神社・大字四条字山小路の天神杜）を{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}し、村名をとって巽神社と改称したものである。 ===式内・横野神社=== 現在地の北東約1キロメートルの生野区巽西3丁目9に「横野神社跡」がある。かつては「印地宮」と呼ばれていた池の小祠であったが、{{jalink|1731年|Q6963}}（享保16年）2月18日に式内・横野神社であるとして横野神社に改称し、祭神を{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の第二皇子の印色入日子命と定めた。『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』に仁徳天皇13年に横野堤を築いたとの記述があり、この堤の鎮守であったものとみられる。 === 外部リンク === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html 巽神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:たつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

Name=Tatsumi Shrine | Image=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Osaka City|Q35765}}{{jalink|Ikuno Ward|Q490520}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) | Main hall={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{jalink|Ikuno Ward|Q490520}}, {{jalink|Osaka City|Q35765}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} as the principal {{jalink|kami|Q11591100}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{jalink|Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|Tatsumi Village}} of {{jalink|Nakakawachi District|Q11366394}} (formerly {{jalink|Shibukawa District|Q11561609}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|consolidation}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{jalink|1731|Q6963}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. The {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tatsumi Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

former site|WD=Q135185614}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府大阪市生野区巽南三丁目17番19号', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:巽神社 (大阪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Tatsumi Shrine | Image=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Osaka City|Q35765}}{{jalink|Ikuno Ward|Q490520}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) | Main hall={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{jalink|Ikuno Ward|Q490520}}, {{jalink|Osaka City|Q35765}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} as the principal {{jalink|kami|Q11591100}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{jalink|Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|Tatsumi Village}} of {{jalink|Nakakawachi District|Q11366394}} (formerly {{jalink|Shibukawa District|Q11561609}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|consolidation}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{jalink|1731|Q6963}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. The {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tatsumi Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]]

Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Osaka City|Q35765}}{{jalink|Ikuno Ward|Q490520}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) | Main hall={{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 15|Q2919}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{jalink|Ikuno Ward|Q490520}}, {{jalink|Osaka City|Q35765}}. It enshrines {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} as the principal {{jalink|kami|Q11591100}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{jalink|Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|Tatsumi Village}} of {{jalink|Nakakawachi District|Q11366394}} (formerly {{jalink|Shibukawa District|Q11561609}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|consolidation}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{jalink|1731|Q6963}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}. The {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tatsumi Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]]

link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Tatsumi Shrine | Image=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tatsumi Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪市の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Tatsumi Shrine | Image=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Tatsumi Shrine | Image=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

link|Q11479957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Tatsumi Shrine | Image=[[File:Tatsumi Shrine Osaka.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Torii gate and worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}} Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome 17-19 | Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|30.2|N|135|33|14.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity={{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Tatsumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tatsumi Shrine''' (たつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tatsumi-Minami 3-chome, {{ill|lt=Ikuno Ward|Q490520|WD=Q490520|1=Ikuno-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as the principal {{ill|lt=kami|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}}, and also enshrines Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ===History=== On April 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), the Hachiman Shrine (enshrining Emperor Ōjin) that had been located within {{ill|lt=Tatsumi Village|Q11479954|WD=Q11479954|lt=Tatsumi Village|1=Tatsumi}} of {{ill|lt=Nakakawachi District|Q11366394|WD=Q11366394|1=Nakakawachi district}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Shibukawa District|Q11561609|WD=Q11561609|1=Shibukawa district}}) was merged with five shrines in the village (the Yokono Shrine in Ōaza Daichi, Aza Inji (a Shikinaisha, enshrining Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto); the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Ikaga, Aza Ikaga; the Amateru Kōdaijin Shrine in Ōaza Nishiashiro, Aza Yoshinouchi; the Kumano Shrine in Ōaza Yagara, Aza Miyanomae; and the Tenjin Grove in Ōaza Shijō, Aza Yamagōji) through {{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=consolidation|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}, and was renamed Tatsumi Shrine after the village name. ===Shikinaisha and Yokono Shrine=== About one kilometer northeast of the current location, at Tatsumi-Nishi 3-chome 9 in Ikuno Ward, there is a “Site of Yokono Shrine.” It was formerly a small shrine on a pond called “Inji-no-miya,” but on February 18, {{ill|lt=1731|Q6963|WD=Q6963|1=1731}} (Kyōhō 16), it was identified as the Shikinaisha Yokono Shrine and renamed accordingly, with its enshrined deity designated as Inshiki-iri-hiko no Mikoto, the second imperial prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. The {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} records that the Yokono embankment was constructed in the 13th year of Emperor Nintoku’s reign, and the shrine is believed to have served as the guardian deity of this embankment. === External links === * [https://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/tatsumi_jinja/index.html Tatsumi Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto footer}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tatsumi Shrine (Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:生野区の歴史]] [[Category:生野区の建築物]] [[Category:巽 (大阪市)]]
